Novel Influenza A Virus Infections 
If human infection with a novel influenza A virus of animal origin (e.g., avian influenza A virus or swine influenza A virus) is suspected, the local and state health department should be contacted to perform RT-PCR for seasonal influenza viruses, influenza A virus subtyping, and novel influenza A viruses. Most commercially available influenza diagnostic tests do not specifically detect novel influenza A viruses. CDC has partnered with some commercial laboratories to expand avian influenza A(H5) testing capacity in the United States. More information is available at Laboratory and Testing Information for Novel Influenza A Viruses | Bird Flu. 
Information about novel influenza A viruses is available at:
Novel Influenza A Virus Infections 2024 Case Definition 
Laboratory and Testing Information for Novel Influenza A Viruses
